Why do you need to specifically eject a flash drive before removing it from the computer?

column3: To make sure there are no viruses being transferred to the flash drive.
column5: Viruses don't play a direct part here.

column3: To flush the memory buffer between the computer and the flash drive.
column5: Correct!

column3: To flush the memory buffer on the flash drive so no data is lost on the computer.
column5: The memory buffer is on the main system or the computer, not the flash drive.

column3: To calculate how big the finished file will be on the flash drive.
column5: This is done automatically by the operating system as the file is read onto the flash drive.

column2: MC
Flash drives are considered to be ____________________.

column3: multi media
column5: multi media would be videos, animations, and audio files. These could be stored on a flash drive but the flash drive itself is not considered to be multi media.

column3: external media
column5: Correct. Any external storage device such as hard disks, flash drives, or USB connections to cameras are all considered multi media.

column3: mobile devices
column5: mobile devices are cell phones or tablet computers.

column3: always connected to the system
column5: Nope. The beauty of a flash drive is that it can easily be disconnected from the system and carried around.

column3: meta data
column5: meta data is data about data. The structure of a file would be meta data about that file.

column2: MC

column3: You may lose the information on your computer.
column5: The data remains on your computer. It is the data in the buffer that could be lost before it is written to the flash drive.

column3: There may still be data in the buffer which hasn't been transferred to the flash drive and it will be lost.
Everything on the flash drive could be deleted.
column5: No, only the information in the temporary buffer.

column3: Specific information that you just typed would be lost from your computer and your flash drive. It is not a pretty sight.
column5: No, only the information in the temporary buffer.

column3: All external media must be ejected properly to move the data from the temporary buffer onto the external device. A flash drive like an external hard drive is considered external media.
